findings.description: The PDF presents data in a wide single-page table layout, but the HTML conversion breaks the table into narrow columns with extensive row wrapping, significantly altering the visual layout and making the table much harder to read.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a wide table with all columns visible in a single row. HTML page 1 shows the same data but with Owner-Facility names and Case Numbers wrapping extensively, creating much taller rows and a compressed column layout.

findings.description: Table data is preserved but cell content wraps extensively in the HTML version due to narrow column widths, making rows much taller than in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF rows are compact single-line or two-line entries. HTML rows for the same data span 4-7 lines due to text wrapping in the Owner-Facility column.

findings.description: Same layout compression issue continues on page 2 of the PDF vs page 3-4 of the HTML, with the wide table format being compressed into narrow columns.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 maintains the same wide table format. HTML pages 3-4 show the continuation with the same narrow column wrapping issue.

findings.description: The 'Total Cases: 40' summary line is preserved in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: Both PDF page 2 and HTML page 4 show 'Total Cases: 40' at the bottom of the table.

findings.description: No h1 document title is present in the HTML. The document title 'DWQ January 2013 Civil Penalty Assessments' appears only in the page-header div, not as a heading element.
findings.evidence: The PDF title 'DWQ January 2013 Civil Penalty Assessments' is placed in a <div class="page-header"> rather than an <h1> tag. No heading elements (h1-h6) are used at all in the HTML.

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> element inside another <main> element, which is a structural issue but does not duplicate content.
findings.evidence: The outer <main> wraps the entire body content, and an inner <main> wraps the table. This is a structural HTML issue, not a content duplication issue.
